STAGES

Tap any stage to battle there with its themed enemy deck. Each stage has a unique opponent crafted from that stage's franchise or theme.

HOW TO PLAY

OBJECTIVE

You and the CPU each have 3 towers:

  • Tower L & Tower R (side towers, 150 HP each)
  • King Tower (250 HP, in the middle)

How to win:

  • You must destroy at least one side tower before you can attack the King
  • Unpaired fighters attack the nearest enemy tower
  • Once a side is open, fighters in that lane attack the King
  • Destroy the King Tower to win — or score 8 KOs

BOARD LAYOUT

Two lanes (L and R), each 3 slots wide:

  • Tower strip: Enemy towers (top) and your towers (bottom)
  • 3 rows per side: BACK (deploy) → NEUT → BATTLE
  • 6 slots per row: fighters fill left→right automatically
  • Fighters advance forward each turn within their slot
  • Combat: fighters pair up left→right against enemies in front

HOW TO DEPLOY

  1. Tap a fighter card in your hand to select it
  2. Tap a + slot in BATTLE row or BACK row — card auto-fills the first open slot left→right
  3. The middle (neutral) row is LOCKED — fighters only arrive there by advancing
  4. Each fighter costs energy (1-9). Energy starts at 3 and grows +1 per turn (max 10).
  5. Tap blue END TURN card when done

PROGRESSION

Each turn, fighters march toward the battle row:

  1. Within a row, units slide LEFT (slot 2 → slot 1 → slot 0)
  2. The leftmost unit (slot 0) advances to the next row, entering at the rightmost slot (slot 2)
  3. Back row → (Neutral row) → Battle row

ATTACK RANGE

Most fighters are melee (range 1) — they must be in the battle row to fight. Zoners have range 2, shown by a blue R2 badge. They can fire from the neutral row without advancing — perfect for staying safe behind a tank.

TOWERS FIGHT BACK

Towers aren't passive — each turn, they fire at enemy fighters in range:

  • Side towers (L, R): 1 damage to an enemy in the battle row
  • King tower: 2 damage to lowest-HP enemy in battle row
  • Fragile fighters (like Pichu) die fast to sustained tower fire

SPEED (SPD) MATTERS

When two fighters clash, the one with higher SPD attacks first. The event log shows who struck first and why. Speedsters can often land a kill before taking any damage.

20-CARD DECK & DRAW

Each battle uses a 20-card deck built from your team x2 (16 fighter cards) plus 4 standard items. You start with 5 cards in hand, draw 1 card per turn, and can hold up to 7 cards at a time.

ITEM CARDS

Your deck includes 4 standard items every battle. Tap an item, then tap a target unit (highlighted gold):

  • 🧪 Health Potion (cost 3) — +2 HP to a friendly unit
  • ⚔️ Beam Sword (cost 4) — +1 ATK to a friendly unit (next combat)
  • 💣 Bomb (cost 5) — 2 damage to an enemy unit
  • ⚡ Energy Drink (cost 2) — +4 energy this turn (instant, no target)

KING TOWER SIEGE

The King tower is locked behind the side towers (L and R). You must destroy at least one side tower before:

  • Your fighters can attack the King
  • The King can fire counter-attacks at you

This creates a proper siege rhythm — break a wall first, then go for the throne.

COMBAT

Fighters advance toward each other each turn. When they meet between battle zones, they fight! Higher SPD attacks first. Damage rolled on D6 — Pokémon TCG style!

Tower attacks: A fighter in the battle row with no enemy in their lane attacks the enemy tower for their ATK value. The King takes -1 damage. Towers don't fight back.

REFERENCE

D6 COMBAT ROLLS

  • Roll 1-5: Glancing hit
  • Roll 6-15: Standard hit
  • Roll 16-19: Strong hit
  • Roll 6: CRITICAL! ATK+1, ignores defense

STAT EFFECTS

  • HP: Health. KO'd at 0.
  • ATK: Base damage (1-3).
  • SPD: Higher speed strikes first.
  • WT: Defense — reduces incoming damage.
SUPER SMASH BROS TCG · v2.4.0
